screens.name: Draft design token foundation
screens.purpose: Define a concrete draft token baseline for the design rule engine that matches the current warm restrained editorial utility aesthetic.
screens.primary_action: Establish reusable draft token values for current and future UI work

ux_rules: Derive the first token baseline from the current repository aesthetic profile and working songs surface instead of inventing a new visual direction.
Use spacing, contrast, weight, and rhythm as the primary hierarchy tools before introducing stronger accent color or ornamental treatments.
Keep the token baseline concrete enough to guide implementation while preserving unresolved choices as explicit notes instead of false precision.
Favor a restrained warm editorial tone that still supports prosumer scanning and operational clarity.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Represent the design rule engine token baseline as a concrete draft contract bound to the current Tailwind styling layer, not as an abstract wishlist.
interaction_risks: A token baseline that is too decorative may weaken utility surfaces.
A token baseline that stays too vague will not reduce one-off styling decisions in future work.
Current product surfaces may still contain local values that do not align cleanly with the new draft baseline.
notes: This run defines system design assets rather than a new end-user screen.
The songs workspace is the primary reference surface for current repo aesthetic binding.

constraints: Update the existing token template rather than introducing a second token contract file.
Keep the binding source as the current repo Tailwind layer and existing CSS variables for this pass.
Define concrete draft values for spacing, typography, color, radius, shadow, and grid with notes where the choice is still provisional.
Do not broaden the scope into a full UI migration unless later workflow steps explicitly require it.
open_decisions: Whether display typography should remain fully Geist-based or later bind to a dedicated editorial display face remains open.
Whether accent hue should trend more olive-neutral or brown-neutral should remain draft-level rather than fully locked.
Whether current product surfaces should later be normalized against the token baseline in a separate implementation pass remains open.

critical_judgments: The draft token values should clearly express the warm restrained editorial utility profile.
Typography and spacing choices should create hierarchy through rhythm and weight rather than decorative flourish.
The resulting token contract should be usable by builders working in the current Tailwind-based repo.
must_not_regress: Current token-source binding to the repo styling layer
Operational clarity in internal utility surfaces

visual_constraints: Avoid cold sterile minimalism, saturated accent breakouts, and luxury-signaling that obscures utility.
Keep neutrals warm, low-saturation, and paper-like rather than bluish or chrome-like.
Prefer restrained radius, rare shadows, and typography-led hierarchy.
Use a finite spacing scale and repeatable baseline rhythm instead of one-off nudges.
